name: NotebookLM
description: NotebookLM Enterprise (part of Gemini Enterprise) provides programmatic creation and management of NotebookLM notebooks on Google Cloud.

auth_methods.note: Docs instruct creating service accounts, using gcloud to obtain access tokens, and show Authorization: Bearer <access_token>; this indicates OAuth2 bearer tokens via service accounts or user credentials. 'service_account' mapped to 'other' to fit schema; 'none_public' included to reflect no anonymous/no API-key-only access.

credential_access.note: Setup docs show enabling the API and creating service accounts in a Google Cloud project (which is self-serve if you have a project and billing). However the product page surfaces enterprise/contact-sales messaging and there is no published self-serve NotebookLM Enterprise signup or pricing; it's unclear whether NotebookLM Enterprise access is gated by enterprise sales for most customers or available to self-serve Google Cloud projects with billing enabled.

api_surface.note: API reference documents REST endpoints for creating, getting, patching, and deleting notebooks and includes request/response schemas, indicating a broad REST surface.

buildability.note: Although REST API and auth mechanisms are documented and code examples use gcloud access tokens, public product messaging directs enterprise customers to contact sales and there's no clear public self-serve signup/pricing for NotebookLM Enterprise. The main blocker is uncertainty whether a developer can obtain usable NotebookLM Enterprise credentials without engaging Google Cloud sales or enterprise enrollment.
